eligibility.summary: Any two available raiders.

beats.text: You have been quiet since we came in.
Just sorting the useful thoughts from the loud ones.
Keep the useful ones. The rest can wait.

outcome.summary_template: {observer} checked in with {subject}.

safe_fallback.text: We can talk when the thought is ready.

eligibility.summary: Ordinary camp ambience.

beats.text: The camp feels settled tonight.

variants.text: Nothing is on fire that should not be. A fine evening.

beats.text: That usually means someone is planning tomorrow.
Then let them plan. We can have one quiet minute.

outcome.summary_template: {observer} and {subject} shared a quiet moment in camp.

safe_fallback.text: A quiet camp is worth noticing.

eligibility.summary: Two raiders sharing the communal fire.

beats.text: You took the last crisp ration.
I took responsibility for it.
A burden carried bravely.
The Writ will remember my sacrifice.

outcome.summary_template: {teaser} teased {target} by the campfire.

safe_fallback.text: Save me one next time.

eligibility.summary: Two raiders sharing the forge.

beats.text: You keep reaching for the large hammer.
It makes the problem reconsider its position.
The problem is a buckle.
Then it should reconsider quickly.

outcome.summary_template: {teaser} joked with {target} while they worked at the smith.

safe_fallback.text: Pass the small hammer when you are done.

eligibility.summary: Two raiders working at the apothecary.

beats.text: Red cord means observed. White cord means only suspected.
And no cord?
No one has checked the label. Start there.

outcome.summary_template: {teacher} showed {learner} the apothecary's labeling system.

safe_fallback.text: Label it before we move on.

eligibility.summary: A shared training or formation activity.

beats.text: Your third step keeps crossing the marker.
Only when you crowd the lane.
Then take it clean before I get there.
Again. This time watch closely.

outcome.summary_template: {challenger} and {partner} challenged each other during training.

safe_fallback.text: Again from the marker.

eligibility.summary: Two raiders studying the current target.

beats.text: Someone marked this timing three different ways.
Three attempts, or three opinions?
That is the useful question.

outcome.summary_template: {reader} compared archive notes with {partner}.

safe_fallback.text: Mark it as uncertain for now.

eligibility.summary: A recent active-roster change.

beats.text: Your marker is back on the active board.
For this attempt. I know how quickly it can move.
Then make this attempt the one they remember.
I will. Thank you for saying it plainly.

outcome.summary_template: {encourager} encouraged {discouraged} after the roster changed.

safe_fallback.text: You earned another attempt.

eligibility.summary: A recruitment visit with two available raiders.

beats.text: You found the quarters. That is the difficult part of the first night.
I assumed the difficult part was learning nineteen names.
Twenty, once they learn yours.
Then I had better start.

outcome.summary_template: {veteran} welcomed {recruit} into the Writ.

safe_fallback.text: Welcome to the Writ.

eligibility.summary: A victory return to camp.

beats.text: The last call landed before anyone needed it repeated.
That is what a clean finish sounds like.
Remember it before the next pull makes us humble again.

outcome.summary_template: {witness} and {partner} reflected on a successful raid.

safe_fallback.text: That finish is worth remembering.

eligibility.summary: A wipe return and a recent combat failure.

beats.text: I moved when the warning came. It was still late.
Then the warning is not your cue. Find the breath before it.
You think the pattern starts earlier?
I think we can look for it together.

outcome.summary_template: {speaker} discussed the last failed mechanic with {confidant}.

safe_fallback.text: We will look at the timing together.

eligibility.summary: Any available pair; uncommon because of frame and pair cooldowns.

beats.text: I keep expecting the camp to feel temporary.
It is temporary. That does not make the people temporary.
You make that sound simple.
Only because I have had time to practice saying it.

outcome.summary_template: {speaker} confided in {confidant} about life in the Writ.

safe_fallback.text: You do not have to carry that alone.

eligibility.summary: At least one direction has 18 respect.

beats.text: You corrected the lane before I called it.
You were busy pretending not to need the help.
Next pull, I see it first.
Good. I would hate to win this argument cheaply.

outcome.summary_template: {rival_a} and {rival_b} renewed their friendly rivalry.

safe_fallback.text: Next pull, I see it first.

eligibility.summary: Two raiders sharing the reflection bench.

beats.text: I remember the mistake more clearly than the recovery.
Then remember who helped you recover.
That is a kinder record.

outcome.summary_template: {speaker} reflected with {listener} in the quarters.

safe_fallback.text: Keep the recovery too.

eligibility.summary: The rememberer has an earlier mechanic thread.

beats.text: You found the step before the warning this time.
I heard what you said after the last attempt.
Good. Keep the part that worked.
And bring it back next pull.

outcome.summary_template: {partner} recognized {rememberer}'s progress on a difficult mechanic.

safe_fallback.text: You improved. Keep that part.

eligibility.summary: The rememberer has an earlier roster memory.

beats.text: You stopped checking the active board every hour.
Only because I finally believed the marker would still be there.
And if it moves again?
I will still know where I belong.

outcome.summary_template: {rememberer} told {companion} they were adjusting to the roster change.

safe_fallback.text: The board is not the whole Writ.

eligibility.summary: The rescuer has an earlier rescue thread.

beats.text: You still look surprised when someone mentions that save.
I remember how close it was, not how well it ended.
Both are true. You are allowed to keep the second part.
I am trying.

outcome.summary_template: {witness} helped {rescuer} reflect on an earlier rescue.

safe_fallback.text: The rescue mattered.

eligibility.summary: An eligible introducer and a learner without this lore topic.

beats.text: The old arena ledgers call twenty fighters a full writ.
So our name is not ceremonial.
No. The tally was logistical: twenty entrants, each given a mark.
And the marks without a return?
Left blank. The ledger records what happened; it does not invent an answer.

outcome.summary_template: {teacher} taught {learner} about the Beast Crucible's old arena ledgers.

safe_fallback.text: The ledgers define a full writ as twenty marked fighters.

eligibility.summary: A qualified skeptic and a learner without the disputed account.

beats.text: Three towns claim their founders built the Crucible.
Which claim does the archive accept?
None. The surviving ledgers begin after the arena was already operating.
So the honest answer is that we do not know.
That is the only account all three towns dislike.

outcome.summary_template: {skeptic} explained the disputed origins of the Beast Crucible to {learner}.

safe_fallback.text: No surviving ledger names the first keepers.

eligibility.summary: An arena or archive source and a learner without the confirmed fact.

beats.text: Linked rings on an intake plaque marked an arena handler, not an entrant.
The chains were an occupation mark?
On the plaques, yes. The ledgers list handlers separately from every writ.
Then the symbol survived after its meaning was forgotten.

outcome.summary_template: {teacher} taught {learner} what the Crucible's linked-ring marks meant.

safe_fallback.text: Linked rings marked handlers, not entrants.
